en: Avetta Kazakhstan Annual Update

The average number of employees shall be calculated on an individual basis and indicate the number of full-time positions according to the conditions applicable at the workplace. IMPORTANT: Please make sure to all include Full-Time, Part-Time and Contract workers under your supervision, even if they were not under your direct employment. Reporting period Jan 01-Dec 31 Entrepreneur Code of the Republic of Kazakhstan:
Please enter the Average Number of Employees your company had during the given year:

Include hours worked by salaried, hourly, part-time and contract workers including temporary help services workers. Do not include vacation, sick leave, holidays, or any other non-work time, even if employees were paid for it. If your establishment keeps records of only the hours paid or if you have employees who are not paid by the hour, please estimate the hours that the employees actually worked. If this number isn't available, an estimate is still needed.\nReporting period Jan 01-Dec 31
Please enter the total number of hours worked by all employees during the given year. Please round your answer to the next highest whole number.

### High Workhours: 

Are your hours and/or employee count entered correctly? The total number of hours reported are considerably *HIGHER* than the average annual hours typically worked by full-time employees. 

Please validate that you have entered your correct employee count and/or total hours. If correct, provide a brief explanation for approval (this will be visible to your client connections). Otherwise, please correct your answers.

### Low Workhours:  

Are your hours and/or employee count entered correctly? The total number of hours reported are considerably *LOWER* than the average annual hours typically worked by full-time employees. 

Please validate that you have entered your correct employee count and/or total hours. If correct, provide a brief explanation for approval (this will be visible to your client connections). Otherwise, please correct your answers.
Kazakhstan Labor Code (Adilet Article 1(27) Kazakhstan Bureau of National Statistics—statistical bulletin “On work-related injuries and occupational diseases”
Did your company record/report any occupational incidents during the reporting year including fatalities, injuries resulting in disability, occupational diseases, poisonings, or first aid?
Yes
No
These include death of an employee resulting from an accident at work or occupational disease, while performing work duties or employer’s tasks. Kazakhstan Labor Code, Article 1(27) \n\t\n
Please enter the number of fatalities for the given year.
These include irreversible impairment of a worker’s ability to work due to occupational injury or disease. \nLaw of the Republic of Kazakhstan "On social protection of persons with disabilities"
\t\n
Please enter the number of incidents resulting in permanent disability for the given year.
These include temporary loss of capacity to work, typically for at least one working day, due to a workplace injury, illness, or other incident. \nKazakhstan Labor Code
\t\n
Please enter the number of incidents resulting in temporary disability for the given year.

These include chronic or acute disease caused by exposure to harmful or hazardous factors during performance of official work duties. Kazakhstan Labour Code (Adilet Article 1(51)) Kazakhstan Ministry of Health Rules
Please enter the number of occupational diseases for the given year.

These include poisoning of an employee caused by harmful workplace exposures, typically chemicals, reported as a cause/outcome in annual injury statistics. \nKazakhstan Bureau of National Statistics Quality Report
\t\n
Please enter the number of poisonings for the given year.
These include incidents requiring only basic first aid (no lost work time or restricted duty). Kazakhstan Rules for Provision of First Aid by Non-Medical Persons
Please enter the number of first aid incidents for the given year.
